Title: The trans-Golgi-localized protein BICAT3 regulates manganese allocation and matrix polysaccharide biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: matrix polysaccharides; pectin; partially methyl-esterified homogalacturonan
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: shoot cell wall; pollen tube cell wall (subapical region/apex)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BICAT3 promotes proper Mn allocation to the trans-Golgi and supports matrix polysaccharide/pectin biosynthesis; loss of BICAT3 reduces pectin-related wall sugars and homogalacturonan deposition, impairs pollen tube growth and seed set, but increases chloroplast Mn and improves photosynthesis under Mn deficiency.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BICAT3 localizes mainly to the trans-Golgi and is ubiquitously expressed, including pollen and pollen tubes. 2. Heterologous yeast assays showed BICAT3 mediates Mn2+ and Ca2+ transport. 3. Arabidopsis bicat3 mutants are hypersensitive to Mn deficiency and Ca toxicity, and under Mn deficiency display altered shoot cell wall monosaccharide/linkage composition with reduced GalA, Gal, Xyl and defective pectin deposition. 4. bicat3 pollen tubes show reduced/heterogeneous JIM5-labeled homogalacturonan, abnormal swelling/branching, shorter siliques and reduced seed set. 5. Loss of BICAT3 increases chloroplast Mn and improves PSII performance under Mn deficiency, indicating altered inter-organelle Mn allocation.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UPF0016 family bivalent cation transporter; Arabidopsis homolog of TMEM165/GDT1
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitous; embryo, root, leaves, stem, flowers, pollen grains and pollen tubes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitous; embryo, root, leaves, stem, flowers, pollen grains and pollen tubes
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Mn deficiency; Mn toxicity; Ca toxicity; Ca deficiency
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native BICAT3 promoter (used for complementation and Venus fusion); ProBICAT3 (native, endogenous/reporter)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout/knockdown mutants; native-promoter complementation; subcellular localization by confocal microscopy and immunogold; yeast complementation; ion quantification in shoots/roots/chloroplasts; cell wall monosaccharide and glycosidic linkage analyses; pollen tube growth assays; JIM5 immunostaining; reciprocal crosses; grafting
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of BICAT3 causes reduced vegetative growth under Mn deficiency, curled leaves, altered cell wall composition, aberrant pollen tube growth, shorter siliques and diminished seed set; however, it improves photosynthetic performance and chloroplast Mn accumulation under Mn deficiency.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BICAT3 is a candidate target to improve Mn-use efficiency and cell wall glycan traits, but manipulation must balance improved chloroplast Mn/photosynthesis under Mn deficiency against strong penalties in growth, fertility, and wall integrity. Tissue- or condition-specific engineering rather than complete loss of function would be safer.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall matrix polysaccharides and pectin support cell expansion, wall mechanics, water binding, pollen tube elongation and fertility.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BICAT3 is classified here as a Common Gene because it functions primarily as a Golgi Mn2+/Ca2+ transporter rather than a catalytic pathway enzyme or transcriptional regulator. The paper distinguishes its function from ECA3; they partly cooperate in vegetative Mn efficiency but are not redundant, and ECA3 does not explain the pollen phenotype.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Golgi-dependent cell wall matrix polysaccharides in vegetative tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ECA3 contributes to vegetative performance under Mn deficiency, but its physiological role is distinct from BICAT3 and it does not substantially affect pollen-related fertility phenotypes described for BICAT3.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. eca3 mutants are hypersensitive to low Mn2+ supply in vegetative tissues. 2. The bicat3 eca3 double mutant shows exacerbated vegetative growth defects under Mn deficiency, indicating partial cooperation but not redundancy. 3. eca3 siliques are not shortened, supporting a role distinct from BICAT3 in reproduction/pollen Golgi Mn supply.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Very low in pollen according to cited expression data
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Very low in pollen according to cited expression data
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant and double-mutant phenotyping under Mn deficiency; silique analysis; photosynthesis and elemental measurements in single and double mutants
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation exacerbates vegetative Mn-deficiency sensitivity, especially in combination with bicat3.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving Mn efficiency in vegetative tissues, but not a substitute for BICAT3 for reproductive cell wall/glycan processes; stacking or coordinated modulation with other Golgi Mn transport components would require caution.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports Golgi-dependent glycosylation and matrix polysaccharide production needed for growth under Mn limitation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core comparison gene because the Results section experimentally analyzes eca3 and bicat3 eca3 double mutants to define BICAT3 function.
